source_metadata.title_raw: Metropolitan Water District of Southern CA - A-18-153 - October 16,  2018 - Los Angeles

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
. 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
October 16, 2018 
Metropolitan Water District of Southern CA 
Gerald Riss, Interim Ethics Officer 
Marcia L. Scully, General Counsel 
PO Box 54153 
Los Angeles, CA 90054-0153 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-153 
Dear Mr. Riss and Ms. Scully: 
This letter responds to your request on behalf of Metropolitan Water District of Southern 
California ("Metropolitan") board member Michael Camacho for advice on Government Code 
Section 1090, et seq. 1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest 
provisions of Section 1090, not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common 
law conflict of interest, including Public Contract Code. 
Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby ( 197 5) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Los Angeles County 
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.l(c)(3).) We did not receive a . 
written response from either entity. (Section 1097.l(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, 
for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1 ( c )( 5).) 
QUESTION 
Does Section 1090 preclude Metropolitan contracts with Pacifica Services, Inc. ("Pacifica") 
while Director Camacho is on the board? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. As explained below, Section 1090 would prohibit such contracts, as Director Camacho 
would be financially interested in any contracts involving Pacifica. 
1 Government Code Sections 1090 through 1097.5. All statutory references are to the Government Code, 
unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 
through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of 
the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-18-153 
Page No. 2 
Metropolitan is a regional wholesaler that delivers water to its 26 public member agencies -
14 cities, 11 municipal water districts, and one county water authority - which in tum provide water 
to 19 million people in Los Angeles, Orange, Riverside, San Bernardino, San Diego and Ventura 
counties. Metropolitan is governed by a 38-member board of directors ("Metropolitan Board"), 
which represents their respective member agencies. 
Contracting Authority at Metropolitan 
In the course of its duties, the Metropolitan Board votes on various types of contracts, 
including construction and professional consulting services contracts. Some Metropolitan 
contractors are selected through a competitive process and thereafter approved by the Board, 
whereas certain contracts valued at $250,000 or less, including professional services contracts, are 
awarded under the Metropolitan General Manager's codified authority and are not subject to Board 
action or review. 
Director Michael Camacho 
Director Camacho joined the Metropolitan Board in 2009. He currently chairs the Real 
Property and Asset Management Committee; is Vice Chair of the Organization, Personnel and 
Technology Committee; and is a member of the following Metropolitan Board Committees: 
Agriculture and Industry Relations, Communications and Legislation, Legal and Claims, and 
Engineering and Operations. As a member of the Metropolitan Board, Director Camacho must 
file statements of economic interests pursuant to Section 87200. 
Pacifica Services, Inc. 
Director Camacho is employed by Pacifica, a Pasadena-based, full-service engineering, 
environmental services, and project and construction management firm founded by his father, CEO 
and President, Ernest Camacho. Pacifica has served the public and private sectors for approximately 
40 years in fields including energy, education, infrastructure, water, and wastewater. In past years, 
Pacifica has developed environmental impact reports and provided construction, management, 
inspection, and other services for other California water districts. 
Pacifica has approximately 60 employees. Although staff size varies over time, Pacifica 
consistently maintains ten or more employees on staff. Director Camacho has been employed by 
Pacifica since the 1980s and currently serves as one of its two Senior Vice Presidents. His duties 
include the management of Pacifica staff and large scale projects unrelated to the water industry. He 
is also identified as a director and officer in Pacifica's articles of incorporation. Director Camacho 
has continuously received an annual salary and a year-end holiday bonus, the amount of which 
remains static each year based on the employee's salary level and position. He has no other personal 
financial interest in Pacifica beyond his annual salary and year-end bonus (no ownership, stock, 
commission income, business trust assets, etc.). His spouse has no financial interest in Pacifica. 


Metropolitan and Pacifica - Contracts 
File No. A-18-153 
Page No. 3 
Pacifica does not currently have any current or pending contracts with Metropolitan. 
Pacifica also has no history of contracting with Metropolitan as a sub or prime consultant or 
contractor. However, earlier this year, a prime consultant included Pacifica as a named 
subconsultant in response to a Request for Proposals to provide professional services for 
Metropolitan. Director Camacho was not involved in formulating the proposal and did not 
participate in Metropolitan's evaluation process. Director Camacho was not aware that Pacifica 
had been included as a subcontractor in the proposal. Ultimately, the prime consultant substituted 
another subconsultant for Pacifica, and a different prime consultant was later awarded the contract. 
Nevertheless, Pacifica's inclusion as a subconsultant in the initial proposal prompted further 
review of potential conflicts, particularly with respect to Section 1090. Because Pacifica may 
reappear as a named subcontractor in a future contract bid, or be involved in another potential 
contracting scenario with Metropolitan, Director Camacho and Metropolitan are proactively seeking 
advice to determine whether Section 1090 would preclude such contracts while Director Camacho 
is on the Metropolitan Board. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 1090 provides, in part, that "[m]embers of the Legislature, state, county, district, 
judicial district, and city officers or employees shall not be financially interested in any contract 
made by them in their official capacity, or by any body or board of which they are members." 
Section 1090 applies to virtually all state and local officers, employees, and multi-member bodies 
whether elected or appointed and covers a broad range of actions by a public official other than 
actually approving a contract, including actions involving preliminary discussions, negotiations, 
compromises, reasoning, planning, drawing plans and specifications, and solicitation for bids. 
In this instance, Director Camacho is a public officer subject to the provisions of Section 
1090. Additionally, as a member of the Metropolitan Board, he is presumed to be involved in the 
making of all contracts by Metropolitan irrespective of whether he actually participated in the 
making of the contract. (Thomson v. Call, (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 645 & 649.) Thus, the 
determinative question is whether he has a financial interest in a contract if Metropolitan contracts 
with Pacifica. 
Under Section 1090, "the prohibited act is the making of a contract in which the official has 
a financial interest" (People v. Honig, (1996) 48 Cal. App. 4th 289, 333), and officials are deemed 
to have a financial interest in a contract if they might profit from it in any way. (Ibid.) Although 
Section 1090 does not specifically define the term "financial interest," case law and Attorney 
General Opinions state that prohibited financial interests may be indirect as well as direct, and inay 
involve financial losses, or the possibility of losses, as well as the prospect of pecuniary gain. 
(Thomson, supra, at pp. 645, 651-652; see also People v. Vallerga (1977) 67 Cal.App.3d 847,867, 
fn. 5; Terry v. Bender (1956) 143 Cal.App.2d 198, 207-208; People v. Darby (1952) 114 
Cal.App.2d 412, 431-432; 85 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 34, 36-38 (2002). Employees have been found to 
have a financial interest in a contract that involves their employer, even where the contract would 
not result in a change in income or directly involve the employee because an employee has an 


File No. A-18-153 
Page No. 4 
overall interest in the financial success of the firm and continued employment. (84 
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 158, 161-162 (2001).) 
However, you note that Director Camacho is not only an employee of Pacifica, he is also a 
director and officer of Pacifica. In 65 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 41 (1982),'the Attorney General's Office 
concluded that "service as a director of a [for-] profit corporation renders one 'financially 
interested' within the meaning of section 1090 even in the situation where such service is not 
compensated .... " The opinion noted that all actions of a corporation are exercised by or under 
the ultimate direction of its board of directors whether it is a for-profit corporation (Corp. Code, § 
300(a)) or nonprofit corporation (Corp. Code,§§ 5210, 7210, 9210). 
Therefore, as a director and officer of Pacifica, Director Camacho would have a financial 
interest in any contract with Pacifica. None of the remote interests in Section 1091 or the non-
interest exceptions in Section 1091.5 appear to apply. Section 1090 therefore prohibits Metropolitan 
from entering into any contracts with Pacifica while Director Camacho is on the board. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
ZWN:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Zachary W. Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

Section 1090 provides, in part, that "[m]embers of the Legislature, state, county, district,
